steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The task is to explain an advantage of the Oak Tree House dwelling site specifically. The correct choice must name a concrete benefit tied to Oak Tree House's location — not a neutral fact, and not an advantage that belongs to Yucca House.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The location of Oak Tree House, an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site in southwestern Colorado, provided an advantage to its inhabitants.': Vaguely says the location provided an advantage but never names what that advantage was. Too general to accomplish the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Located in southwestern Colorado, Oak Tree House is an Ancestral Puebloan dwelling site that was inhabited from approximately 1180–1210 CE.': States only neutral facts (location and date range) with no mention of any advantage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The relatively flat terrain on which Oak Tree House was built allowed for the construction of large terraced buildings.': Attributes Yucca House's advantage (flat terrain, large terraced buildings) to Oak Tree House. Factually wrong based on the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Since it was built under a rock overhang, Oak Tree House was naturally protected from heavy rain and snow.': Directly connects Oak Tree House's defining feature (the rock overhang) to a concrete advantage (protection from rain and snow). This is the specific advantage the notes describe. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
